checks.a_analysis: The output contains only headings, paragraphs, and lists that are present in the source document. No fabricated body text, introductions, or conclusions were found.
The output mentions 'NC.gov', which is present in the source text on page 2. No phone numbers, email addresses, or other URLs were found in the source or fabricated in the output.

Both pages of the source document are represented in the output. All major sections (Mission, Vision and Priorities, Funding, Quick Facts, What We Do, Boards and Commissions, Budget, and 2022 Accomplishments) are present.
The source document contains no tables; information is presented in text blocks and bulleted lists which have been correctly converted to list elements.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed both the source PDF and the JSON output. There are no instances of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items. Each content block from the source appears exactly once in the structured output.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. The main titles ('N.C. Department of Information Technology' and '2022 ACCOMPLISHMENTS') are correctly assigned as Heading Level 1. All secondary section headers (MISSION, FUNDING, QUICK FACTS, etc.) are consistently assigned as Heading Level 2. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings.
The source document contains no tables. The information is presented in text blocks and lists. Therefore, no table corruption is possible.

The output follows a logical reading order. For the two-column layout on Page 1, it reads the left column (Mission -> Funding -> Quick Facts) and then the right column (What We Do -> Boards -> Budget -> Vision). This flow is standard for accessibility and accurately reflects the document's intended organization.

All lists are captured correctly. The number of items in each list (Quick Facts, What We Do, Boards and Commissions, Priorities) matches the source exactly. On Page 2, the series of accomplishment paragraphs is grouped into an unordered list; while the source lacks physical bullets, these are clearly discrete items, and representing them as a list is a valid structural interpretation that maintains the document's meaning.
The source document does not contain any hyperlinked text or clearly visible URLs (e.g., clickable web addresses). The JSON output correctly contains no links, reflecting the source content.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output converts the two-column layout of the first page of the PDF into a single-column layout. This is a standard practice for responsive web design and does not confuse the meaning or flow of information. The content follows the logical reading order of the original document (left column then right column from page 1, followed by the full width content of page 2). Navigation elements like the 'Contents' sidebar are expected additions.

There are no formal tables (grids with rows and columns) in the source PDF document. Information is presented using stylized text blocks and bulleted lists, which are faithfully represented as lists and paragraphs in the HTML.

The source PDF contains no meaningful images such as photos, diagrams, or logos. It utilizes decorative elements like solid blue header bars and a pixelated graphic footer on page 2. The exclusion of these decorative elements in the HTML output is an intentional accessibility improvement and not a defect.

There is no duplicated body content in the HTML output. Section headings are listed in the 'Contents' navigation box and then appear as actual headings in the body, which is a standard and expected accessibility feature for document navigation. The core textual content and list items are not duplicated.
